title: Chewy Umami Egyptian Almond Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful North African dish featuring chewy almonds, aromatic spices, and a sweet and tangy sauce. This Egyptian-inspired tagine is perfect for adventurous eaters and plant-based foodies alike.
instructions: Rinse the almonds and soak them in water for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the almonds, then transfer them to a blender or food processor.
Add the dates, lemon juice, ginger, cinnamon, cloves, paprika, and salt to the blender.
Blend the mixture until it's smooth and the almonds are well chopped.
Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it's translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Add the blended almond mixture to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Stir the mixture occasionally to prevent burning.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
